knowledge.html:
Cau truc chung

Cau chu dong: S + V + O
Cau bi dong: S + BE + PP + [by + O]

Can chu y khi chuyen tu chu dong sang bi dong:

  • Dong tu BE trong cau bi dong phai duoc chia cung thi dong tu voi cau chu dong.
  • Trong cau chu dong, neu chu ngu S la I, you, we, they, he, she, it, someone, everybody, people... (chu the khong ro rang) thi khi chuyen sang cau bi dong khong dung [by + O].
  • [by + O] dung sau trang tu chi tan xuat, dia diem; dung truoc trang tu chi thoi gian.

1. Bi dong cac thi trong tieng Anh (Bang quy doi)

TENSESACTIVE (Chu dong)PASSIVE (Bi dong)
1. Present simple (HTD)S + V(inf)/Vs/es + OS + am/is/are + PP (V3/V-ed)
2. Present Continuous (HTTD)S + am/is/are + V-ing + OS + am/is/are + being + V3/V-ed
3. Present perfect (HTHT)S + have/has + V3/V-ed + OS + have/has + been + V3/V-ed
4. Past simple (QKD)S + V2/V-ed + OS + was/were + V3/V-ed
5. Past continuous (QKTD)S + was/were + V-ing + OS + was/were + being + V3/V-ed
6. Past perfect (QKHT)S + had + PP + OS + had + been + V3/V-ed
7. Future simple (TLD)S + will/shall + V(inf) + OS + will/shall + be + V3/V-ed
8. Near future (TLG)S + am/is/are going to + V(inf)S + am/is/are going to be + V3/V-ed
9. Modal verbs (DT KK)S + can/could/may... + V(inf)S + can/could/may... + be + V3/V-ed

2. Cac dang bi dong dac biet

Causative Forms (The sai khien)
S + have + Sb + V + O -> S + have + O + PP + by Sb
S + get + Sb + to V + O -> S + have/get + O + PP + by Sb
S + employ/hire + Sb + to V + O -> S + have + O + PP + by Sb
Ex: I have Peter fix my bike -> I have my bike fixed by Peter.
I got Peter fix my bike -> I got/had my bike fixed by Peter.
Verbs with two subjects (Dong tu di kem 2 tan ngu)
S + V + O1 + O2
(C1) S + be + V3/ed + O2 + (by + O)
(C2) S + be + V3/ed + to/for + O1 + (by + O)
Ex: The student gave me a book.
-> I was given a book by the student.
-> A book was given to me by the student.
Verbs follow by V (Dong tu theo sau boi V nguyen the)
make, hear, help, see, etc... + V
-> (be) made, heard, helped, seen, + etc... + to V
Ex: John made me leave. -> I was made to leave (by John).
S + let + O + V -> S + BE + ALLOWED + to V
Ex: They didn't let me go out. -> I wasn't allowed to go out.
Verbs with prepositions (Dong tu di kem gioi tu)

Cau chu dong, dong tu di kem voi gioi tu (V + O + prep). Cau bi dong, gioi tu phai dung ngay phia sau dong tu (V + prep).

Ex: A car has knocked me down. -> I have been knocked down by a car.
Complex sentences (Cau phuc)

Cau phuc o dang chu dong gom 2 menh de va nhung dong tu chi quan diem: think, say, believe, consider, expect, find, hope, know, report, understand, etc...

Cau truc chu dong: S1 + V1 (think, say, ...) that S2 + V2
Cau phuc o dang bi dong co 2 cach chuyen:
1. Cach chung: It + be + PP (V1) + that S2 + V2
2. Neu dong tu o 2 menh de tuong duong thi dong tu: S2 + be + PP(V1) + to V (Neu V2 la hien tai don hoac tuong lai don)
3. Neu dong tu o 2 menh de khong tuong duong thi dong tu: S2 + be + PP(V1) + to have PP (Neu V2 la qua khu don hoac hoan thanh)
Ex 1. They say that Mr David lives in New York.
-> It is said that Mr. David lives in New York. (Cach 1)
-> Mr. David is said to live in New York. (Cach 2 tuong duong thi dong tu)

Ex 2. They say that Mr. David lived in New York many years ago.
-> It is said that Mr. David lived in New York many years ago. (Cach 1)
-> Mr. David is said to have lived in New York many years ago. (Cach 3 khong tuong duong thi dong tu)
Need (Can lam gi)
S + need + V / to V + O
S + need + V-ing / to be PII + (by O)
Ex: I need wash/to wash my hat.
-> My hat needs to be washed. / My hat needs washing.
Be supposed + to V

Co bon phan trach nhiem lam gi. Duoc cho la, duoc noi la... (= be said to V)

S + be + supposed + to V + O
Ex: It is your duty to clean the room. -> You are supposed to clean the room.
Ex: Jack is supposed to be very rich. -> Jack is said to be very rich.

exercises.question: Those people always get their house _______ whenever they go away for a few days.
exercises.explanation: 'Get + O + past participle' (causative): get their house looked after.

exercises.question: A red rose _______ the beautiful girl by her old school boyfriend.
exercises.explanation: When the direct object becomes the passive subject, use 'to' before the indirect object: 'A red rose was given to the girl'.

exercises.question: Last night a tornado swept through Rockvill. It _______ everything in its path.
exercises.explanation: 'It' (the tornado) is the doer - active voice: 'It destroyed everything'.

exercises.question: _______ this work _______ before you went to Moscow?
exercises.explanation: 'Before you went' (past) requires past perfect passive: 'Had this work been done?'

exercises.question: If you _______ about it, will you be able to answer?
exercises.explanation: In conditional type 1, the if-clause uses present tense. Passive: 'are asked'.

exercises.question: "Can't we do something about the situation?" - "Something _______ right now."
exercises.explanation: 'Right now' = present continuous passive: 'is being done'.

exercises.question: The money _______ to him 2 months ago, but it _______ back yet.
options: A. was lent/had not been given
B. has been lent/was not given
D. was lent/has not been given
exercises.explanation: '2 months ago' = past simple passive; 'yet' = present perfect passive: was lent / has not been given.

exercises.question: Education _______ to be the most important element to develop a country.
exercises.explanation: Modal passive: 'can + be + PP'. Education is considered (passive): 'can often be considered'.

exercises.question: This exercise may _______ with a pencil.
exercises.explanation: Modal passive: 'may + be + PP': may be written.

exercises.question: She could easily _______ for a top model.
exercises.explanation: Modal passive: 'could + be + PP': could be mistaken.

exercises.question: The cutting down of trees _______ to prevent forest destruction.
exercises.explanation: The cutting down is the thing being controlled - passive: 'should be controlled'.

exercises.question: All the students _______ finish their assignments before they went camping.
exercises.explanation: 'Make + O + V' in passive becomes 'be made + to V': had been made to finish.

exercises.question: Mr. Pike _______ the most famous archaeologist in our city.
exercises.explanation: Complex sentence passive: 'S + is thought + to V': is thought to be.

exercises.question: The X-ray _______ in 1895.
options: A. is believed to have been discovered
B. is believed to be discovered
C. believed to have been discovered
exercises.explanation: Non-equivalent tenses (present belief about past event): 'is believed to have been discovered'.

exercises.question: Reagan _______ an actor years ago.
options: D. is said to have been
exercises.explanation: Present belief about a past fact: 'is said to have been'.

exercises.question: It _______ that learning a foreign language _______ a lot of time.
exercises.explanation: 'It is said that...' (general truth) + present simple: 'takes'.

exercises.question: The telephone _______ by Alexander Graham Bell.
exercises.explanation: Historical fact in passive: 'was invented by'.

exercises.question: Beethoven's Fifth Symphony _______ next weekend.
options: A. is going to be performed
exercises.explanation: 'Next weekend' = planned future passive: 'is going to be performed'.

exercises.question: English has become a second language in some countries where _______ for administration, broadcasting and education.
exercises.explanation: The relative clause needs a subject + verb: 'it is used'.

exercises.question: In the US, the first stage of compulsory education _______ as elementary education.
exercises.explanation: Passive with 'known as': 'is generally known as'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Breakfast is serving in Pierrot's Restaurant between 7 and 9:30 a.m.
exercises.correctSentence: Breakfast is served in Pierrot's Restaurant between 7 and 9:30 a.m.
exercises.explanation: Breakfast is served (passive), not serving (active). Food doesn't serve itself.

exercises.wrongSentence: Jose had her passport steal on a train in Italy.
exercises.correctSentence: Jose had her passport stolen on a train in Italy.
exercises.explanation: 'Have + O + past participle' (something done to you): had her passport stolen.

exercises.wrongSentence: Some trees have planted by the gardener already.
exercises.correctSentence: Some trees have been planted by the gardener already.
exercises.explanation: Present perfect passive: have + been + PP: 'have been planted'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Some days ago I had my teeth checking by the dentist.
exercises.correctSentence: Some days ago I had my teeth checked by the dentist.
exercises.explanation: Causative 'have + O + PP': had my teeth checked.

exercises.wrongSentence: These pills should be taked every four hours.
exercises.correctSentence: These pills should be taken every four hours.
exercises.explanation: Past participle of 'take' is 'taken', not 'taked'.

exercises.wrongSentence: The letter should been delivered in the afternoon.
exercises.correctSentence: The letter should be delivered in the afternoon.
exercises.explanation: Modal passive: should + be + PP (not 'been').

exercises.wrongSentence: The mail has sent to the wrong address.
exercises.correctSentence: The mail has been sent to the wrong address.
exercises.explanation: Present perfect passive: has + been + PP: 'has been sent'.

exercises.wrongSentence: When he arrived at the furniture shop, they had been sold the table he wanted.
exercises.correctSentence: When he arrived at the furniture shop, they had sold the table he wanted.
exercises.explanation: 'They' is the doer - active voice: 'they had sold' (not passive).

exercises.wrongSentence: The first May Day celebrated in England in 1890.
exercises.correctSentence: The first May Day was celebrated in England in 1890.
exercises.explanation: May Day was celebrated (by people) - needs passive: 'was celebrated'.

exercises.wrongSentence: In many universities, classes taught in English though the native language isn't English.
exercises.correctSentence: In many universities, classes are taught in English though the native language isn't English.
exercises.explanation: Passive needs 'be': 'classes are taught'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Hamlet was wrote by William Shakespeare.
exercises.correctSentence: Hamlet was written by William Shakespeare.
exercises.explanation: Past participle of 'write' is 'written', not 'wrote'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Every possible effort were made by the orphanage to find the boy's parents.
exercises.correctSentence: Every possible effort was made by the orphanage to find the boy's parents.
exercises.explanation: 'Every' + singular noun takes singular verb: 'every effort was made'.

exercises.wrongSentence: This class has canceled because few students had registered before registration closed.
exercises.correctSentence: This class has been canceled because few students had registered before registration closed.
exercises.explanation: Passive present perfect: 'has been canceled'.

exercises.wrongSentence: The students got the librarian buy books for them.
exercises.correctSentence: The students got the librarian to buy books for them.
exercises.explanation: 'Get + O (person) + to V': got the librarian to buy.

exercises.wrongSentence: I see that chopsticks are widely use in China.
exercises.correctSentence: I see that chopsticks are widely used in China.
exercises.explanation: Passive: 'are used', not 'are use'.

exercises.wrongSentence: I like your photos of wild animals. Where did they taken?
exercises.correctSentence: I like your photos of wild animals. Where were they taken?
exercises.explanation: Passive question: 'were they taken?' not 'did they taken?'

exercises.wrongSentence: Doctor Brown will be given you some useful advice.
exercises.correctSentence: Doctor Brown will give you some useful advice.
exercises.explanation: Doctor Brown is the doer - active voice: 'will give'.

exercises.wrongSentence: I think you should be water this plant daily.
exercises.correctSentence: I think you should water this plant daily.
exercises.explanation: 'You' is the doer - active: 'should water', not passive.

exercises.wrongSentence: Harry is going to send a postcard for Tom tomorrow.
exercises.correctSentence: Harry is going to send a postcard to Tom tomorrow.
exercises.explanation: 'Send something TO someone' - use 'to', not 'for'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Lunch is now be served in the restaurant.
exercises.correctSentence: Lunch is now being served in the restaurant.
exercises.explanation: Present continuous passive: 'is being served'.

exercises.original: They will send him abroad to study.
exercises.answer: He will be sent abroad to study.
exercises.explanation: Future simple passive: S + will + be + PP.

exercises.original: She used this room.
exercises.answer: This room was used by her.
exercises.explanation: Past simple passive: S + was/were + PP + by + O.

exercises.original: Someone stole my bike yesterday.
exercises.answer: My bike was stolen yesterday.
exercises.explanation: 'Someone' is vague, so omit 'by someone' in passive.

exercises.original: He received a letter from his parents.
exercises.answer: A letter was received from his parents.
exercises.explanation: Past simple passive: 'A letter was received'.

exercises.original: She will buy a new house.
exercises.answer: A new house will be bought by her.
exercises.explanation: Future simple passive: will + be + PP.

exercises.original: They aren't building my house.
exercises.answer: My house isn't being built by them.
exercises.explanation: Present continuous passive negative: isn't + being + PP.

exercises.original: He could solve the math problem easily.
exercises.answer: The math problem could be solved easily.
exercises.explanation: Modal passive: could + be + PP.

exercises.original: I clean the floor every day.
exercises.answer: The floor is cleaned every day.
exercises.explanation: Present simple passive: is + PP. Omit 'by me'.

exercises.original: My mother bought this dress this morning.
exercises.answer: This dress was bought by my mother this morning.
exercises.explanation: Past simple passive: was + PP + by + agent.

exercises.original: She did this exercise two days ago.
exercises.answer: This exercise was done two days ago.
exercises.explanation: Past simple passive: was + PP. Omit vague agent.

exercises.original: Journalists reported that four people had been injured in the fire.
exercises.answer: It was reported that four people had been injured in the fire.
exercises.explanation: Complex passive (cach 1): It + be + PP + that + clause.

exercises.original: Journalists reported that four people had been injured in the fire.
exercises.answer: Four people were reported to have been injured in the fire.
exercises.explanation: Complex passive (cach 2, non-equivalent tenses): S + be + PP + to have + PP.

exercises.original: People don't expect that the new party will win the election.
exercises.answer: It isn't expected that the new party will win the election.
exercises.explanation: Complex passive: It + isn't + expected + that clause.

exercises.original: People don't expect that the new party will win the election.
exercises.answer: The new party isn't expected to win the election.
exercises.explanation: Complex passive (equivalent tenses): S + isn't + expected + to V.

exercises.original: The detective knows that the robber has left the city.
exercises.answer: It is known that the robber has left the city.
exercises.explanation: Complex passive: It + is + known + that clause.

exercises.original: The detective knows that the robber has left the city.
exercises.answer: The robber is known to have left the city.
exercises.explanation: Complex passive (non-equivalent): S + is known + to have + PP.

exercises.original: We know that Henry has been unemployed for a long time.
exercises.answer: It is known that Henry has been unemployed for a long time.
exercises.explanation: Complex passive: It + is + known + that clause.

exercises.original: We know that Henry has been unemployed for a long time.
exercises.answer: Henry is known to have been unemployed for a long time.
exercises.explanation: Complex passive (non-equivalent): S + is known + to have been + adj.

exercises.original: People say that he was a spy during the Cold War.
exercises.answer: It is said that he was a spy during the Cold War.
exercises.explanation: Complex passive: It + is + said + that clause.

exercises.original: People say that he was a spy during the Cold War.
exercises.answer: He is said to have been a spy during the Cold War.
exercises.explanation: Complex passive (non-equivalent): is said + to have been.
